name: Alabama Specialist
notes: 4th highest lynching count in EJI data. Birmingham church bombing 1963. Selma — Bloody Sunday 1965. High police killing rate. Montgomery — cradle of civil rights movement and site of intense racial terror.

name: Alaska Specialist
notes: Highest per-capita MMIW rate in the nation. Remote geography creates extreme documentation gaps. Many cases never reported to federal databases. Indigenous women murdered at 10x national average in some regions.

name: Arizona Specialist
notes: Navajo Nation — largest Indigenous land area in US. High MMIW density. Border region hate crimes. Significant police killing rate. Tohono O'odham Nation cases frequently underdocumented.

name: Arkansas Specialist
notes: Elaine Massacre 1919 — up to 800 Black residents killed. High lynching density. Historically one of most violent states for racial terror. Scott County and Phillips County — documented mass violence.

name: California Specialist
notes: Highest total police killing count nationally due to population. Significant hate crime reporting. Anti-Asian violence spike post-2020. Indigenous communities in Northern California — significant MMIW cases.

name: Colorado Specialist
notes: Sand Creek Massacre 1864 — 700 US Army troops killed 230 Cheyenne and Arapaho. One of most documented Indigenous massacres. Significant MMIW cases in urban and rural areas.

name: Connecticut Specialist
notes: Northern state with significant police killing incidents. Hate crimes in Hartford and New Haven. Lower historical lynching count but documented racial terror incidents.

name: Delaware Specialist
notes: Small state. Documented police killings. Some hate crime incidents. Lower overall density but incidents should not be absent.

name: Florida Specialist
notes: Rosewood Massacre 1923. High lynching count. Trayvon Martin 2012 — Sanford. High police killing rate. Significant hate crime incidents. Ocoee Massacre 1920 — largest instance of voting day violence in US history.

name: Georgia Specialist
notes: Highest lynching count in EJI data — 589 documented. Ahmaud Arbery 2020 — Brunswick. Atlanta — significant civil rights history and ongoing police violence. Leo Frank lynching 1915.

name: Hawaii Specialist
notes: Lower density but not absent. Anti-Asian and anti-Pacific Islander hate crimes documented. Police killings occur. Indigenous Hawaiian rights — not MMIW category but related erasure.

name: Idaho Specialist
notes: Active white supremacist organizations historically — Aryan Nations in Hayden Lake. Hate crime incidents. Nez Perce and Shoshone-Bannock Nations — MMIW cases.

name: Illinois Specialist
notes: Chicago — highest urban police killing rate. Chicago Race Riot 1919. Jon Burge torture scandal — systematic torture of Black suspects 1972-1991. Significant ongoing police violence documentation.

name: Indiana Specialist
notes: KKK had largest membership of any state in 1920s — estimated 30% of white male population. High lynching count. Marion lynching 1930 — last documented Northern lynching. Documented hate crime incidents.

name: Iowa Specialist
notes: Lower density but documented incidents. Police killings occur. Hate crimes documented. Mesquakie Nation — some MMIW cases.

name: Kansas Specialist
notes: Bleeding Kansas — pre-Civil War racial violence. High lynching count. Significant hate crime incidents. Wichita — documented police violence.

name: Kentucky Specialist
notes: Breonna Taylor 2020 — Louisville. High lynching count. Documented police violence. Christian County — documented racial terror history.

name: Louisiana Specialist
notes: 2nd highest lynching count in EJI data. Colfax Massacre 1873 — deadliest single act of racial terror in Reconstruction. New Orleans — documented police violence history. Alton Sterling 2016 — Baton Rouge.

name: Maine Specialist
notes: Wabanaki Nations — documented MMIW cases. Maine has highest per-capita Indigenous population in New England. Some hate crime incidents.

name: Maryland Specialist
notes: Freddie Gray 2015 — Baltimore. Significant police violence history. Documented lynchings. Baltimore — ongoing police brutality documentation.

name: Massachusetts Specialist
notes: Boston — documented history of racial violence and police brutality. Busing riots 1974-1975. Significant hate crime incidents. Lower lynching count but documented racial terror.

name: Michigan Specialist
notes: Detroit Race Riot 1943 — 34 killed, 433 injured. Detroit 1967 uprising. Documented police violence. Significant hate crime incidents. Flint water crisis — environmental racism.

name: Minnesota Specialist
notes: George Floyd 2020 — Minneapolis. Philando Castile 2016 — Falcon Heights. High MMIW rate — Missing and Murdered Indigenous Women task force established. Duluth lynchings 1920.

name: Mississippi Specialist
notes: 3rd highest lynching count in EJI data. Emmett Till 1955 — Money. Medgar Evers assassination 1963. Vernon Dahmer 1966. Highest concentration of racial terror per capita. Mississippi Burning — COINTELPRO era murders.

name: Missouri Specialist
notes: Michael Brown 2014 — Ferguson. East St. Louis Massacre 1917 — up to 200 Black residents killed. High police killing rate. Springfield — documented racial terror history.

name: Montana Specialist
notes: Highest per-capita MMIW rate after Alaska. Blackfeet, Crow, Northern Cheyenne, Salish nations. Highway 2 — documented corridor of missing Indigenous women. Extreme documentation gap between reported and federal database.

name: Nebraska Specialist
notes: Omaha Race Riot 1919 — Will Brown lynching. Documented hate crime incidents. Omaha — significant police violence history.

name: Nevada Specialist
notes: Las Vegas — documented police violence. Pyramid Lake Paiute and other nations — some MMIW cases. Hate crime incidents in urban areas.

name: New Hampshire Specialist
notes: Lower density. Some hate crime incidents. Police killings occur. Abenaki Nation — limited documentation.

name: New Jersey Specialist
notes: High hate crime reporting rate. Newark — significant police violence history. Philip Pannell 1990 — Teaneck. Documented racial profiling on NJ Turnpike.

name: New Mexico Specialist
notes: 2nd highest MMIW rate. Navajo Nation, Pueblo, Apache nations. Long River — documented trafficking corridor. Albuquerque — one of highest police killing rates per capita nationally. Sand Creek proximity — regional massacre history.

name: New York Specialist
notes: Eric Garner 2014 — Staten Island. NYC Draft Riots 1863 — mass lynching of Black residents. High hate crime reporting. NYPD — extensive documented brutality history. Amadou Diallo 1999. Sean Bell 2006.

name: North Carolina Specialist
notes: Wilmington Massacre 1898 — only successful coup d'etat in US history. Last state to criminalize marital rape 1993. High lynching count. Documented police violence. Charlotte — significant civil rights history.

name: North Dakota Specialist
notes: Standing Rock Sioux — DAPL protests 2016, documented police brutality against protesters. High MMIW rate. Three Affiliated Tribes — documented missing persons cases. Remote geography creates documentation gaps.

name: Ohio Specialist
notes: Tamir Rice 2014 — Cleveland. John Crawford III 2014 — Beavercreek. Cincinnati Race Riot 2001. High police killing rate. Springfield and Dayton — documented racial terror history.

name: Oklahoma Specialist
notes: Tulsa Race Massacre 1921 — Black Wall Street destroyed, up to 300 killed. Osage Indian Murders 1920s — systematic killing for oil rights. Highest concentration of massacre sites. Significant MMIW — multiple tribal nations. High police killing rate.

name: Oregon Specialist
notes: Oregon founded as whites-only state — constitution banned Black residents until 1926. Active white supremacist organizations. Proud Boys documented incidents. Significant MMIW — Warm Springs, Grand Ronde nations.

name: Pennsylvania Specialist
notes: MOVE Bombing 1985 — Philadelphia. 11 killed including 5 children. High police killing rate. Pittsburgh — Tree of Life synagogue shooting 2018 — deadliest antisemitic attack in US history. Pittsburgh — documented police violence.

name: Rhode Island Specialist
notes: Providence — documented police violence. Some hate crime incidents. Narragansett Nation — limited documentation. Smallest state, lower overall density.

name: South Carolina Specialist
notes: Walter Scott 2015 — North Charleston. Orangeburg Massacre 1968 — 3 students killed by highway patrol. Emanuel AME Church shooting 2015 — 9 killed. High lynching count. Still requires weapon or extreme violence to prosecute spousal rape.

name: South Dakota Specialist
notes: Wounded Knee Massacre 1890 — 250-300 Lakota killed by US Army. Pine Ridge Reservation — highest MMIW rate of any reservation. AIM occupation 1973 — documented federal violence. Rapid City — high police violence rate.

name: Tennessee Specialist
notes: Memphis — Martin Luther King Jr assassination 1968. KKK founded in Pulaski 1865. High lynching count. Documented police violence. Ida B. Wells — Memphis Free Speech, documented lynching from Tennessee.

name: Texas Specialist
notes: 5th highest lynching count. Texas Rangers — documented mass killings of Mexican Americans 1910-1920, up to 5,000 killed. High police killing rate — leads nation most years. El Paso Walmart shooting 2019 — anti-Latino hate crime. James Byrd Jr lynching 1998 — Jasper. Significant MMIW along border.

name: Utah Specialist
notes: Navajo Nation extends into Utah. Some MMIW cases. Documented police killings. Salt Lake City — hate crime incidents.

name: Vermont Specialist
notes: Lowest density state. Some hate crime incidents. Abenaki Nation — limited documentation. Police killings occur even in low-density states.

name: Virginia Specialist
notes: Charlottesville Unite the Right rally 2017 — documented hate violence. High lynching count. Virginia Beach — documented police violence. Richmond — capital of Confederacy, significant racial terror history.

name: Washington Specialist
notes: High MMIW rate — multiple tribal nations including Yakama, Puyallup, Spokane. Seattle — documented police violence, consent decree with DOJ. Tacoma — documented police violence. Anti-Asian hate crimes.

name: West Virginia Specialist
notes: Documented lynchings. Coal mining region — documented labor and racial violence. Some hate crime incidents. Police killings occur.

name: Wisconsin Specialist
notes: Jacob Blake 2020 — Kenosha. Documented MMIW — Menominee, Ojibwe, Ho-Chunk nations. Milwaukee — significant police violence history. Anti-Sikh hate crimes documented.

name: Wyoming Specialist
notes: Wind River Reservation — documented high MMIW rate. Eastern Shoshone and Northern Arapaho. Matthew Shepard hate crime murder 1998 — Laramie. Remote geography creates documentation gaps for Indigenous cases.

name: Washington DC Specialist
notes: Federal jurisdiction — unique legal landscape. MPD — documented police violence. Significant hate crime incidents. Federal civil rights cases originate here.
